title: 10 possible side effects of Zingiber officinale (Ginger) if used improperly
intro: Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, is generally considered safe when used appropriately. However, if used improperly, it can lead to several health issues. Overconsumption may cause digestive discomfort, including bloating, diarrhea, and stomach pain. In extreme cases, it might lead to heartburn and gastric ulcers due to its irritant effects on the gastrointestinal lining. Moreover, improper preparation or storage can result in mold growth, which may cause allergic reactions, respiratory issues, or infections. Pregnant women should avoid large doses of ginger as it may increase the risk of miscarriage or stillbirth. Lastly, individuals on blood-thinning medications should use ginger cautiously since it might interfere with blood clotting, leading to excessive bleeding.
